title: Audio: system design — the algorithmic toolbox and the recognition habit

cues.label: Recognition game
cues.headline: A small toolbox hides in system design
cues.narration: The system-design round is mostly recognition: hear the signal, reach for the tool, defend the trade-off.

cues.label: Modulo fails
cues.headline: Change N and everything reshuffles
cues.narration: Server equals hash modulo N reshuffles almost every key when N changes — a cache-miss storm.

cues.label: The ring
cues.headline: Ownership is local to an arc
cues.narration: Place servers and keys on a ring; a new server only steals one arc, so adding one of N moves about one-Nth of the keys.

cues.label: Token bucket
cues.headline: Lazy refill, bounded burst
cues.narration: Store a count and a timestamp; refill on demand. Bursts up to capacity are allowed, then throttled to the drip rate.

cues.label: Sliding window
cues.headline: Strict rolling count
cues.narration: A sliding-window counter forbids bursts: it counts recent timestamps and allows only if the count is under the limit.

cues.label: Quorum
cues.headline: R + W > N forces overlap
cues.narration: Make read and write sets overlap so a read always sees the latest write; majorities always intersect.

cues.label: Consensus
cues.headline: Majority prevents split brain
cues.narration: Only the side with a strict majority may make progress, so two leaders can never both commit.

cues.label: LRU cache
cues.headline: Map + list = O(1) evict
cues.narration: A hash map plus a doubly linked list gives constant-time lookup, move-to-front, and evict-from-back.
